data.narrative:
Reverion is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Herrmann, Fischer, Weinrich, Hauck, and Poblotzki, currently valued at $310M post-money on $79M total raised. The relevant comp universe consists of four same-stage, same-sector private peers (Pano AI at $500M, Sage Geosystems at $400M, CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M, and Cemvita at $120M), plus one public comparable (LNZA) and five additional private reference points including TAE Technologies.

Two valuation methods triangulate on Reverion's fair value. The peer-median approach identifies a $350M midpoint across the four direct comparables, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the current $310M post-money, implying $589M. These methods produce a median triangulated valuation of $470M, with a low of $298M and a high of $677M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $298M–$677M, centered near $470M. The $310M current valuation sits below the peer median by $40M, suggesting modest upside. Key uncertainties include unit economics and customer concentration, unaddressed in the supplied data, as well as the appropriateness of the 1.9x stage multiplier to this specific capital markets environment.
